name: Ascent to the Peñas Ubinas — circular from Tuíza
name_original: Subida a las Peñas Ubinas — circular desde Tuíza
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lena traverse a mountainous region characterized by significant elevation changes and diverse natural landscapes. The terrain often includes steep ascents and descents, making it suitable for experienced trail runners seeking a challenge. The area features a network of trails that wind through varied environments, from forested paths to open mountain ridges. This geographical composition provides a robust setting for demanding running experiences. Best jogging routes around Lena The most popular jogging route is Las Tres Marías Trail, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain, offering a demanding workout. Another top favourite among local runners is Ascent to the Peñas Ubinas — circular from Tuíza, a difficult 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This running route includes a significant climb, reaching high mountain elevations. Local runners also love the Ricabo to Alto de Ventana Trail — circular via the GR 207, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail leading through varied mountain terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Jogging around Lena is defined by mountainous paths, challenging ascents, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes are geared towards experienced runners. The routes in Lena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Lena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Lena region?
A: The Lena region offers a diverse selection of over 45 running routes. While many are challenging, there are also options for moderate and easy runs.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Lena?
A: While Lena is known for its challenging mountainous terrain, there are a few easier routes available. The guide lists 2 easy and 10 moderate running trails, which could be suitable for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Lena?
A: Jogging routes around Lena traverse a mountainous region with significant elevation changes. You can expect steep ascents and descents, winding through varied environments from forested paths to open mountain ridges. The terrain is generally geared towards experienced trail runners seeking a demanding workout.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Lena?
A: Yes, Lena offers several excellent circular running routes. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240500705/ascent-to-the-penas-ubinas-circular-from-tuiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Peñas Ubinas — circular from Tuíza&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240516498/ricabo-to-alto-de-ventana-trail-circular-via-the-gr-207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricabo to Alto de Ventana Trail — circular via the GR 207&lt;/a&gt; are popular choices that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What are some challenging running trails in Lena for experienced runners?
A: Lena is ideal for experienced trail runners. Notable challenging routes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055968994/las-tres-marias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tres Marías Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1240500705/ascent-to-the-penas-ubinas-circular-from-tuiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Peñas Ubinas — circular from Tuíza&lt;/a&gt;, which reaches high mountain elevations. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Rueda loop from Ḷḷindes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions or viewpoints can I see along the running routes?
A: The Lena region boasts numerous natural attractions and scenic viewpoints. You might encounter stunning mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/176266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventana Pass&lt;/a&gt;. For a picturesque sight, look for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/102603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xiblu Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer panoramic views from summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/859306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de San Salvador&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Lena Lake in Washington State the same Lena as this guide?
A: No, this guide focuses on a mountainous region named Lena, characterized by significant elevation changes and challenging trails, distinct from Lena Lake in Washington State. While Lena Lake offers scenic trail running, the routes described here are in a different geographical context, featuring high mountain passes and varied terrain more typical of the Iberian Peninsula.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Lena?
A: The running routes in Lena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 700 runners have explored Lena's varied terrain, often praising the challenging mountainous paths and scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: Given the mountainous and often challenging nature of Lena's trails, many routes are geared towards experienced runners. However, the 2 easy and 10 moderate routes might offer more suitable options for families looking for less strenuous walks or runs. It's advisable to check individual route details for specific difficulty and length.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Lena?
A: The mountainous character of Lena suggests that late spring, summer, and early autumn are generally the best times for running, offering more stable weather and accessible trails. Winter running might be possible on lower elevation routes, but higher mountain paths could be affected by snow and ice, requiring specialized gear and experience.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Lena?
A: The guide data does not specify dog-friendly policies for all routes. In general, many natural areas in mountainous regions allow dogs, but it's always best to check local regulations or specific route details before heading out with your pet, especially in protected areas or on trails that might be part of a national park.
tips:
The Las Ubiñas-La Mesa Natural Park is a protected area in the Cantabrian Mountains, encompassing parts of Asturias and León. The Peña Ubiña massif is located within this park, which includes several notable peaks, including Peña Cerreo.

Peña Cerreo has an altitude of approximately 2,101 meters. A popular route to ascend Peña Cerreo begins in Tuiza de Arriba, passing through the Meicín Refuge. This route offers spectacular views of the Ubiña massif and is quite accessible for hikers.

Peña Ubiña Pequeña is a mountain located in the Cantabrian Mountains, in the Ubiña Massif, between Asturias and León. At approximately 2,194 meters above sea level, it is one of the most prominent peaks in the area.

The route to climb Peña Ubiña Pequeña is quite popular among mountaineers and can be started from several points, such as Casa Mieres or Tuiza de Arriba.
